seoTitle: West Hoxton vs West Kempsey — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between West Hoxton and West Kempsey. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: West Hoxton vs West Kempsey: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in West Hoxton and West Kempsey.
heading: West Hoxton vs West Kempsey — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between West Hoxton, New South Wales and West Kempsey, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: West Kempsey has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to West Hoxton (45/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is West Hoxton or West Kempsey safer from flooding?
faq.answer: West Hoxton has a flood risk score of 26/100, while West Kempsey scores 28/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: West Hoxton scores 62/100 for bushfire risk. West Kempsey scores 41/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in West Hoxton or West Kempsey?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. West Kempsey has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.2% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
